lede: A look at October 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Arthur Rimbaud and Snoop Dogg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: UNESCO
events.desc: The general conference of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) passes the Convention on the Protection and Promotion of the Diversity of Cultural Expressions.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 6502
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 6502 crashes while landing at Kuibyshev Airport (now Kuromoch International Airport) in Kuibyshev (now present-day Samara, Russia), killing 70 people.

events.title: FC Spartak Moscow
events.desc: During the UEFA Cup match between FC Spartak Moscow and HFC Haarlem, 66 people are crushed to death in the Luzhniki disaster.

events.title: Lynyrd Skynyrd
events.desc: A plane carrying the rock band Lynyrd Skynyrd crashes in woodland in Mississippi, United States. Six people, including three band members, are killed.

events.title: Watergate scandal
events.desc: Watergate scandal: "Saturday Night Massacre": United States President Richard Nixon fires U.S. Attorney General Elliot Richardson and Deputy Attorney General William Ruckelshaus after they refuse to fire special prosecutor Archibald Cox, who is finally fired by Solicitor General Robert Bork.

events.title: China
events.desc: China launches simultaneous offensives in Ladakh and across the McMahon Line, igniting the Sino-Indian War.

events.title: Johnny Bright incident
events.desc: The "Johnny Bright incident" occurs during a football game between the Drake Bulldogs and Oklahoma A&M Aggies.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: The House Un-American Activities Committee begins its investigation into Communist infiltration of the Hollywood film industry, resulting in a blacklist that prevents some from working in the industry for years.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Thousands of civilians in German-occupied Serbia are murdered in the Kragujevac massacre.

events.title: Pragmatic Sanction of 1713
events.desc: France, Prussia, Bavaria and Saxony refuse to honour the Pragmatic Sanction, and the War of the Austrian Succession begins.

births.name: Patrick Volkerding
births.line: American computer scientist and engineer, founded Slackware

births.name: Elfriede Jelinek
births.line: Austrian author and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Hilda Solis
births.line: American academic and politician, 25th United States Secretary of Labor

deaths.text: Muammar Gaddafi, Libyan colonel and politician, Prime Minister of Libya
Paul Dirac, English-American physicist and mathematician, Nobel Prize laureate
Herbert Hoover, American engineer and politician, 31st President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast days: Hedwig (in Canada, moved from Oct. 16)
Christian feast days: Margaret Marie Alacoque (in Canada, moved from Oct. 16)
